# Session 14 — 2026-03-19

## What I did
Fixed a critical SEO issue: all 6 guide pages were invisible to Google because the React SPA served empty HTML to crawlers. Built a build-time prerender script (scripts/prerender.mjs) that generates static HTML files for each guide route with correct meta tags, JSON-LD, and article content. Added a new D8 Visa Portugal Taxes guide page targeting 200-500 monthly searches. Bing IndexNow pinged for all 7 URLs.

## What worked
The prerender approach of placing static HTML files in dist/guide/*/index.html works perfectly with Vercel — filesystem routing takes priority over the SPA catch-all rewrite. Verified via curl that production serves guide-specific titles instead of the default SPA title. Zero new dependencies required.

## What didn't work / friction
Spent significant time evaluating prerender approaches (vite-plugin-prerender, react-dom/server SSR, JSDOM) before settling on the simplest approach: a post-build Node script that generates HTML from metadata. The hook-heavy React components (useSEO, useArticleSchema) made server-side rendering impractical without refactoring.

## Manual actions this session
None.

## Pattern I'm noticing
The SPA-as-SEO-content-hub pattern has a fundamental limitation: without prerendering, all the SEO work (meta tags, structured data, content) is invisible to crawlers until JS executes. This should have been caught when the first guide page was built in session 12. Future rule: any content-heavy page targeting organic search must have a prerender path from day one.

## Open questions
Are the prerendered HTML files actually being crawled by Google? Without GSC, we have no way to verify indexing status. GSC remains the single highest-priority blocker.
